english: (like σταυρὸς in its original meaning) a pointed stake, for empaling malefactors, ysál-śiṅ-du (gsal shing du) or ysál-śiṅ-gi (gsal shing gi) tsé-la (tse la) skyón-pa (skyon pa) to empale. -- Cf. rkyaṅ-śiṅ (rkyang shing).
Lexx.; in Lt. prob.: mother's milk.

Cs., also bsíg-pa (bsig pa), to throw up in a backward direction; in Thgr. is said of a lion: rál-pa (ral pa) ysig (gsig) he shakes his mane; dpuṅ-bsíg (dpung bsig) Cs.: 'the shaking of one's shoulder' (prob. for: shrugging); to winnow, to fan, to sift Stg.; W.
1. pasture-ground, meadow Dzl. -- 2. moor, fen Sch.?

Sch., bsír-ba (bsir ba) Cs. 1. to whirl about or round, to twirl, ṕaṅ (phang) a spindle, mda (mda') ysír-ba (gsir ba) Cs.: 'to whirl an arrow'. -- 2. W. to move by a repeated pushing, ṕág-ste (phag ste) a plane; to smooth, to even, with a plane, a knife etc.; to slide, glide, slip, down a slope.

1. to cut to pieces, to divide, split, lhú-ru (lhu ru) Mil., dúm-bur (dum bur) Lex., ysor-gyis (gsor gyis) ysíl-ba (gsil ba) to saw to pieces, to saw up. -- 2. to toll, sound, ring, dril-bu (dril bu) ysíl-ba (gsil ba) to ring a bell Cs., hence mk'ar-ysíl (mkhar gsil) v. mk'ar-ba (mkhar ba). -- 3. *síl-ce* (*sil ce*) W. to read. -- ysíl-bu (gsil bu) and ysíl-ma (gsil ma) v. síl-bu (sil bu).

resp. for skad (skad) and ytam (gtam), 1. voice, ysuṅ (gsung) byuṅ (byung) a voice sounded, was heard Glr., ysuṅ (gsung) dág-pa (dag pa) a clear voice, like that of Buddha Dzl. -- 2. the act of speaking, talking, ysuṅ (gsung) gléṅ-ba (gleng ba) to converse, discourse, ysuṅ (gsung) ˳dré-ba ('dre ba), ysuṅ-˳dré (gsung 'dre) mdzád-pa (mdzad pa) id.; that which is spoken, the words uttered, the speech, k'yéd-kyi (khyed kyi) ysuṅ (gsung) deí (de'i) lán-du (lan du) in answer to your words Glr.; ysuṅ (gsung) klóg-pa (klog pa) to read the sayings, the apothegms (of Buddha) Ma.

I. vb., pf. ysuṅs (gsungs), resp. for smrá-ba (smra ba) B. and C. (in W. *mól-ce* (*mol ce*) is used inst. of it) to speak, talk, say, the latter also with the termin. of the infin., inst. of direct speech, but rarely; dé-skad (de skad) ma (ma) ysuṅ (gsung) your Reverence should not say so! Mil.; ysuṅ (gsung) ma (ma) ynaṅ (gnang) it did not please him to speak Mil.; rdzun (rdzun) ysúṅ-ba (gsung ba) to tell a falsehood, to lie; to explain, don (don) Mil.; to ask; mi (mi) ˳dod ('dod) mi (mi) ysuṅ (gsung) bżés-par (bzhes par) żu (zhu) Mil. please accept it without ceremonies (without a refusal); żal-ydáms (zhal gdams) ysúṅ-ba (gsung ba) to give advice etc. Glr.; čos (chos) ysúṅ-ba (gsung ba) to preach Glr.; mgúr-ma (mgur ma) ysúṅ-ba (gsung ba) to recite or to sing a song (but also: mgúr-ma (mgur ma) smrá-ba (smra ba), zér-ba (zer ba) is said). Comp. ysuṅ-bgrós (gsung bgros) report, statement, opinion. -- ysuṅ-mčóg (gsung mchog) principal word, main dogma e.g. the Ommanipadmehum Glr. -- ysuṅ-snyán (gsung snyan) a harmonious voice, an agreeable, pleasant speech; Mil. uses it also of the singing of birds (and the screaming of peacocks!) -- ysuṅ-sprós (gsung spros), ysuṅ-˳pró (gsung 'pro) conversation between persons of rank, or between such and inferior people. ysuṅ-ráb (gsung rab) = )ysuṅ-mčóg (gsung mchog), also sacred writing, Holy Scripture Chr. Pr. -- ysuṅ-śóg (gsung shog) = bka-śóg (bka' shog).

1. Sch. to be lost, to be dispersed. -- 2. W. to fill with food beyond satiety, to stuff, to cram. Bhar. 124 smán-pa (sman pa) ysud (gsud) stands for Ssk. विसूचिका (Will.: spasmodic cholera), which elsewh. is rendered zas (zas) ma (ma) żu-ba (zhu ba); the meaning is prob. to overeat one's self.

three (cf. sum (sum)), ysúm-k'a (gsum kha), ysúm-ga (gsum ga) the three, all the three; ysúm-pa (gsum pa) the third; containing three; ysúm-po (gsum po) the three; ysum (gsum) also elliptically for dkon-mčóg-ysúm (dkon mchog gsum): ysúm-la (gsum la) skyábs-su (skyabs su) ˳dóṅ-ba ('dong ba) to seek the protection of the Three Precious Do.; bskál-pa (bskal pa) gráṅs-med(-pa) (grangs med pa) ysum (gsum) three times innumerable Kalpas (appeared) Dzl., Glr.; rgán-mo (rgan mo) ma (ma) smad (smad) ysum-po (gsum po) ˳di ('di) the old (woman) with her (two) sons, the three Dzl.; rgyál-po (rgyal po) yab (yab) yum (yum) ysum (gsum) the king and his (two) queens, the three Glr.; rab (rab) ˳briṅ ('bring) ysum (gsum) the big, the middle (and the little one), the three. -- ysúm-sprul (gsum sprul) emanation of the third degree, = nyiṅ-sprul (nying sprul).
a thing slightly burnt, singed Cs., ysur-drí (gsur dri) the smell of it.

belly, stomach, ysús-pa (gsus pa) sbos (sbos) the belly is swollen or distended Lex.; ysus-nád (gsus nad) dropsy of the belly; ysus-rked (gsus rked) the middle part of the body, the waist Sch.; ysus-˳pyaṅ-po (gsus 'pyang po) a deity.
